name: Helen Humes
abstract: Helen Humes (June 23, 1913 – September 13, 1981) was an American singer. She was a blues, R&B and classic popular singer.

works.t: If I Could Be with You (One Hour Tonight)
Song of the Wanderer (Where Shall I Go)

quotes.text: And I said, "oh my goodness I haven't even hummed since I've been home. I can't do nothing". He said, "yes you can Helen. Now you can sing." I said, "well it's been so long I don't know whether I can sing or not". He said, "well why don't you come on and try". I said, "because Carnegie Hall is too big to be trying to sing in". And so he said, "well, just come on, you can make it". And I said, "well, alright then, if you think I can make I'll come on and sing something". So I went on and Nellie Lutcher, bless her heart, she came and she said, "Helen I'll stay right here with you until you go on". She said, "don't be scared". And I said, "well you know every time I get nervous and I get hoarse and everything". She ran across the street to the drug store and she brought me more cough drops and cough syrups and stuff. And I said, "well if I ain't feeling better now, I won't". And I went on and it was sensational.
quotes.caption: Helen Humes' nervous debut at From Spirituals to Swing

quotes.text: Yeah, Stanley came there and he sat there almost all night talking and telling me, "Helen you ought to come on back". And I told him, "I aint even hummed". And he said, "but you can sing. You can still sing". And I said, "well, I'll try but Carnegie Hall is too big a place to be trying to sing in".
quotes.caption: Helen Humes recalls hesitation about singing at Carnegie Hall

about_quotes.speaker: Ernie Fields
about_quotes.evidence: During that time I did a short tour with Helen Humes, she was a feature.

about_quotes.speaker: Buck Clayton
about_quotes.evidence: And we had picked up Helen Humes... I do know Helen was in the Famous Door... pictures of me and Joe Jones and Jimmy Rushing and Helen Humes

about_quotes.speaker: Gerald Wiggins
about_quotes.evidence: when I was with singers I did quite a bit of travel, especially with Helen Humes, we used to go to Europe every year for George Wein.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gerald Wiggins interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 2, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Red Norvo
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with Helen after -- later... we get Helen Humes, and a trio, we went to Australia for about three months.

about_quotes.speaker: Jo Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Who came in the band? Helen Humes, 1938, July. ... Helen stayed with the band, as far as I know - '40, '41, '42

about_quotes.speaker: Freddie Green
about_quotes.evidence: Helen -- I think she sounded good with the band. I think she fit with the band
